pattern: Sort both greed factors and cookie sizes, then greedily satisfy the least greedy remaining child with the smallest cookie that fits, maximizing the number of content children.
hints: A cookie that satisfies a greedy child also satisfies a less greedy one, so spend your smallest cookies on the children easiest to please.
Sort children by greed and cookies by size, then walk both with two pointers.
Advance the cookie pointer until a cookie meets the current child's greed; when it does, count that child and move to the next child. Stop when either list runs out.

edge_inputs.description: first example, only one of three children can be satisfied
second example, more cookies than children
no cookies means no content children
every cookie too small for any child
single child and single fitting cookie
largest cookie wasted if greedily mismatched

stress.description: large greed and cookie lists with overlapping ranges so a non-trivial subset is satisfiable
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 30000))
    m = rng.randint(0, n)
    g = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(n)]
    s = [rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(m)]
    return [g, s]
stress.note: overlapping value ranges keep the greedy matching meaningful
